key_insight: The paper combines a high-order continuous FEM with a fine low-order invariant-domain scheme and graph-based convex limiting to preserve physical bounds for nonlinear scalar conservation laws.
engineering_relevance: This offers a robust way to get high accuracy in smooth regions while preventing nonphysical oscillations near shocks, which is valuable for reliable transport and wave simulation codes.
method: High-order residual-viscosity continuous FEM for the primary update.
Low-order invariant-domain scheme on a fine P1 submesh induced by nodal points.
Graph-Poisson flux reconstruction plus convex limiting and capacity-based mass redistribution to enforce conservation and bounds.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on smooth and nonsmooth scalar conservation laws showing high-order accuracy, robustness near discontinuities, and convergence to the entropy solution.
watch_item: The capacity-based redistribution and flux limiting may add implementation complexity, especially in cases where admissible capacity is tight and conservation-preserving correction is difficult.
paper_title: High-Order Invariant-Domain Preserving Continuous Finite Elements via Graph-Poisson Convex Limiting
